posts.text: "A female agent wearing a gaiter-style mask rolled down the window, leaned out — and addressed Emily by name.  “She yelled, ‘Emily, Emily, we're going to take you home!’ Then she looked at her phone and she recited my home address,” she says.”

...and took a picture of me and my car,” she says.  Emily says she decided to leave at that point, but the SUV made a sudden U-turn and barreled towards her, braking next to her driver's side window."

"Emily, who NPR is only identifying by her first name because she fears retribution from the federal government, says she followed an ICE vehicle at a safe distance into a parking lot. “And then someone leaned out of the passenger side of that SUV... 

northcountrypublicradio.org/…

Always unnerving to see people treating war like its a sports game, supporting their teams like it isn't something with extraordinary destruction that, under the BEST circumstance, those who haven't been obliterated will need to spend many decades attempting to rebuild from.
